SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.storage

Class NInteger

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNField
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNElementary
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNNumeric
com.nib.commons.storage.NInteger
All Implemented Interfaces:
IDataArea, IntegerVariable, Cloneable
Direct Known Subclasses:
MqLong, NInt16, NInt32, NInt64, NPointer, NUInt16, NUInt32
public class NInteger extends AbstractNNumeric implements IntegerVariable
The NInteger class provides an implementation for handling numeric integer variables. It is derived from the AbstractNNumeric base class and can handle both signed and unsigned integers with configurable endianness. This class is typically used for memory representations and manipulation of integers. Key features include: - Support for both big-endian and little-endian formats. - Ability to specify signed or unsigned integers. - Configurable default values and precision through `digits`. Constructors: - Constructors allow initialization with memory references, lengths, signedness, and endianness. Endianness Operations: - Handles byte ordering through methods such as setLong and getLong. - Supports both big-endian and little-endian manipulations via respective helper methods. Value Management: - Offers various setter and getter methods to handle initialization, retrieval, and setting of integer values in different formats (int, long, double, BigDecimal). - Default values and zero initialization can be specified. Formatting: - Provides a customizable string representation of the value with predefined width (`digits` field). Memory Interaction: - The class uses the `Memory` and `IDataArea` objects for managing memory addresses and data storage interactions. Note: - The internal routines for signedness adjustments may require review for correctness. - This class assumes a fixed byte-width representation for values (e.g., 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes).

  • Constructor Details

    • NInteger

      public NInteger(Memory memory, int offset, int length, boolean signed, Endianness endianness)
      Constructs an NInteger object with the specified parameters.
      Parameters:
      memory - the memory object that backs this NInteger.
      offset - the offset within the memory where this NInteger begins.
      length - the length, in bytes, of this NInteger.
      signed - a flag indicating whether this NInteger is signed.
      endianness - the byte order (endianness) of this NInteger.

    • NInteger

      public NInteger(Memory memory, int offset, int length)
      Constructs an NInteger object with the specified parameters.
      Parameters:
      memory - the memory object that backs this NInteger.
      offset - the offset within the memory where this NInteger begins.
      length - the length, in bytes, of this NInteger.

    • NInteger

      public NInteger(int length)
      Constructs an NInteger object with the specified length. This constructor initializes the object with a default signed flag set to true and the specified endianness as BIG_ENDIAN.
      Parameters:
      length - the length, in bytes, of this NInteger.

    • NInteger

      public NInteger(int length, boolean signed)
      Constructs an NInteger object with the specified length, signed flag, and default endianness.
      Parameters:
      length - the length, in bytes, of this NInteger.
      signed - a flag indicating whether this NInteger is signed.

    • NInteger

      public NInteger(int length, boolean signed, Endianness endianness)
      Constructs an NInteger object with the specified length, signed flag, and endianness.
      Parameters:
      length - the length, in bytes, of this NInteger
      signed - a flag indicating whether this NInteger is signed
      endianness - the byte order (endianness) of this NInteger

    • setLong

      public void setLong(long value)
      Updates the current instance with the provided long value based on the configured byte order and length of the instance. The value is stored in big-endian or little-endian format depending on the instance's endianness, and handles various lengths such as 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes.
      Parameters:
      value - the long value to set in the instance
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the length of the instance is not one of the supported values

    • getLong

      public long getLong()
      Retrieves the long value representation of the current NInteger instance based on its configured byte order (endianness) and length. The value is extracted as a signed or unsigned number depending on the object's attributes. This method supports data lengths of 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes.
      Specified by:
      getLong in class AbstractNNumeric
      Returns:
      the long value represented by the current instance. The value interpretation depends on the instance's length, endianness, and signed attributes.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the length of the instance is not one of the supported values.

    • getInt

      public int getInt()
      Description copied from class: AbstractNNumeric
      Gets the value of the numeric field as an integer.
      Specified by:
      getInt in interface IntegerVariable
      Specified by:
      getInt in class AbstractNNumeric
      Returns:
      the integer value of the numeric field

    • setInt

      public void setInt(int value)
      Description copied from interface: IntegerVariable
      Sets the integer value.
      Specified by:
      setInt in interface IntegerVariable
      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to set

    • resetDefaultValue

      protected void resetDefaultValue()
      Description copied from class: AbstractNElementary
      Resets the field to its default value. Implementations of this method should define the specific logic required to restore the field to a predefined state or value. This method is typically invoked during memory allocation or field reinitialization processes.
      Specified by:
      resetDefaultValue in class AbstractNElementary

    • getHalfWordBE

      BIG ENDIAN / MAINFRAME LIKE

      protected int getFullWordBE(boolean signed)

    • setFullWordBE

      protected void setFullWordBE(int value, boolean signed)

    • getDoubleWordBE

      protected long getDoubleWordBE(boolean signed)

    • setDoubleWordBE

      protected void setDoubleWordBE(long value, boolean signed)

    • getHalfWordLE

      protected int getHalfWordLE(boolean signed)
      LITTLE ENDIAN

    • setHalfWordLE

      protected void setHalfWordLE(int value, boolean signed)

    • getFullWordLE

      public int getFullWordLE(boolean signed)
      Retrieves a 32-bit integer from the memory at the specified address in little-endian byte order. The integer may be interpreted as signed or unsigned depending on the provided input.
      Parameters:
      signed - a flag indicating whether the retrieved integer should be interpreted as signed or unsigned.
      Returns:
      the 32-bit integer value reconstructed from the memory in little-endian byte order.

    • setFullWordLE

      public void setFullWordLE(int value, boolean signed)
      Sets a 32-bit integer value in little-endian byte order at the memory location specified by this object. The value is split into four bytes and stored starting at the offset retrieved from the address associated with this instance.
      Parameters:
      value - the 32-bit integer value to set
      signed - a flag indicating whether the stored value should be interpreted as signed

    • getDoubleWordLE

      public long getDoubleWordLE(boolean signed)
      Retrieves a 64-bit integer from the memory at the specified address in little-endian byte order. The integer may be interpreted as signed or unsigned depending on the provided input.
      Parameters:
      signed - a flag indicating whether the retrieved 64-bit integer should be interpreted as signed or unsigned.
      Returns:
      the 64-bit integer value reconstructed from the memory in little-endian byte order.

    • setDoubleWordLE

      public void setDoubleWordLE(long value, boolean signed)
      Sets a 64-bit integer value in little-endian byte order at the memory location specified by this object. The value is split into eight bytes and stored starting at the offset retrieved from the address associated with this instance.
      Parameters:
      value - the 64-bit integer value to set
      signed - a flag indicating whether the stored value should be interpreted as signed
